name: Eddie Bert
abstract: Edward Joseph Bertolatus (May 16, 1922 – September 27, 2012), also known as Eddie Bert, was an American jazz trombonist.

interviews.title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

quotes.text: Lincoln Center and the Town Hall. I mean he did a Carnegie Hall but they never recorded that.
quotes.caption: Eddie Bert recalls an unrecorded Carnegie Hall concert by someone else
quotes.doc_title: Eddie Bert interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Manny Albam
about_quotes.evidence: In the band was Shorty Rodgers, let me see, the trombone player DeVito, Eddie Bert, a whole bunch of all young kids in the band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Manny Albam interviewed by Monk Rowe, Tarrytown, New York, October 31, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: Alan Raph
about_quotes.evidence: Eddie and I have done a lot of work together. ... We were the only two trombones. We are sitting side by side
about_quotes.doc_title: Alan Raph interviewed by Monk Rowe, Danbury, Connecticut, November 20, 2001

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: met Eddie Bert down at the Village and we'd become friends. And so I formed a little band

about_quotes.speaker: Jones Hank
about_quotes.evidence: We recorded with Eddie Bert, a trombonist and some porn players also.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jones Hank — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Charlie Barnet
about_quotes.evidence: Well, he was in the band at the same time that Trummy Young was.

about_quotes.speaker: Giacomo Gates
about_quotes.evidence: Bill Finnegan, Eddie Bert, people that you interviewed that I watched the interviews and listened. I just felt like wow, I was inspired by them and by being able to partake in what they were doing.
about_quotes.doc_title: Giacomo Gates interviewed by Monk Rowe, Utica, New York, November 11, 2017 [interview 2 of 2]
